user.title: Equipment
user.description: Select your thermostat and one or more weather sources. The first weather entity is primary; additional ones serve as fallbacks if the primary is unavailable.

data_description.monitor_only: Run the full optimization pipeline without writing setpoints. Safe to point at a thermostat already controlled by another instance.

setup_complexity.title: Setup Mode
setup_complexity.description: Choose how much to configure. You can upgrade from minimal to full at any time in the integration options.

data_description.setup_complexity: Minimal uses weather forecasts to shift HVAC runtime to efficient hours. No sensors to add, no model to train — it starts optimizing immediately. Full adds a physics-based thermal model that learns your home over weeks for more precise scheduling.

thermal_profile.title: Thermal Model
thermal_profile.description: Choose how to initialize the optimizer's thermal model of your home. Learning mode works for everyone; a Beestat profile speeds things up.

data_description.initialization_mode: "Learn automatically" starts fresh and takes 2-3 weeks to fully calibrate using conservative settings. "Import Beestat profile" uses your measured data for faster startup (~3-5 days). "Restore exported model" loads a previously saved model.

thermal_profile_beestat.title: Beestat Profile Import
thermal_profile_beestat.description: Provide the path to your Beestat Temperature Profile JSON file.

data_description.profile_path: Full path inside your Home Assistant instance — no quotes needed around spaces. For HAOS/Docker: upload the file to your config directory (e.g. via Samba or File Editor add-on) and enter /config/beestat_profile.json. For Core installs, use the absolute filesystem path.

thermal_profile_import.title: Restore Exported Model
thermal_profile_import.description: Paste the JSON data from a previously exported model.

data_description.model_import_data: Paste the JSON from the export_model service.

air_sensors.title: Air Sensors
air_sensors.description: Optional indoor and outdoor sensors that significantly improve the optimizer's accuracy. Detected sensors are pre-filled — adjust as needed. You can skip this step and configure sensors later.

presence_setup.title: Presence Detection
presence_setup.description: Optional person or presence entities for home/away detection. When everyone is away, the optimizer widens the comfort range to save more energy.

hvac_specs.title: HVAC System Specs
hvac_specs.description: Optional details about your HVAC system. These improve cold-start accuracy so the optimizer makes better decisions from day one. Skip if unsure — the model will learn these automatically.

data_description.home_sqft: Approximate conditioned square footage of your home.
data_description.hvac_tonnage: Rated tonnage of your heat pump or AC unit. Check the model number plate or your installer's paperwork.
data_description.aux_heat_type: Type of backup/auxiliary heat: electric resistance strips, gas furnace, or none.
data_description.aux_heat_kw: Rated capacity of your auxiliary heat source in kilowatts. For gas furnaces, enter the electrical equivalent.

comfort.title: Temperature Boundaries
comfort.description:
The optimizer saves energy by shifting when your HVAC runs.

COOLING: pre-cool toward the minimum during efficient hours, coast up toward the maximum during expensive hours.
HEATING: pre-heat toward the maximum during efficient hours, coast down toward the minimum during expensive hours.

A wider range means more savings. Safety limits are absolute guardrails that are never exceeded.

data_description.safety_heat_min: Absolute floor. Your home will never be cooler than this, even when away or during demand response. Example: 45°F.
data_description.safety_cool_max: Absolute ceiling. Your home will never be warmer than this, even when away or during demand response. Example: 90°F.
data_description.comfort_cool_min: Pre-cool target during efficient hours (e.g. cool mornings). Example: 72°F.
data_description.comfort_cool_max: How warm you'll accept during expensive/inefficient afternoon hours. Example: 78°F.
data_description.comfort_heat_min: How cool you'll accept during expensive/inefficient hours. Example: 62°F.
data_description.comfort_heat_max: Pre-heat target during efficient hours (e.g. mild afternoons). Example: 70°F.

error.sensor_overlap: A sensor cannot be used as both indoor and outdoor — please remove the duplicate
error.profile_not_found: File not found — check the path is accessible inside your HA container (no quotes needed around spaces)
error.profile_parse_error: Could not parse file as JSON — verify the file is a valid Beestat temperature profile export
error.profile_missing_keys: Missing required data — the file needs temperature deltas (cool_1, heat_1, resist) with linear trendlines and balance points
error.invalid_model_data: Invalid model data — paste the JSON from the export_model service
error.cool_range_inverted: Cooling minimum must be less than cooling maximum
error.heat_range_inverted: Heating minimum must be less than heating maximum
error.safety_above_comfort: Safety minimum must be below the comfort heating minimum
error.comfort_above_safety: Comfort cooling maximum must be below the safety maximum

mode.title: Operating Mode
mode.description: Toggle between active control and monitor-only observation.

data_description.monitor_only: When enabled, the optimizer runs the full pipeline (forecasting, scheduling, EKF learning) but never writes setpoints. Useful for running a second instance side-by-side for model comparison.

equipment.title: Thermostat & Weather Source
equipment.description: Change your thermostat or weather source without losing learned thermal model data. The optimizer will reload with the new entities.

data_description.aux_heat_override_entity: A binary sensor or input_boolean that turns on when auxiliary/emergency heat is running. Use this if your thermostat doesn't report aux heat status. Create a template binary_sensor in Home Assistant with your detection logic (e.g., heating + high duct temperature) and select it here.

outdoor_sensors.title: Outdoor & Building Sensors
outdoor_sensors.description: Optional outdoor and building envelope sensors that improve prediction accuracy. Detected sensors are pre-filled — adjust as needed.

energy.title: Energy & Cost Tracking
energy.description: Configure power monitoring, electricity rates, and carbon intensity to enable savings tracking and cost-aware optimization. Detected entities are pre-filled where possible.

data_description.hvac_power_entity: A sensor that reports your HVAC system's current power draw in watts.
data_description.hvac_seer: Enter the SEER1 rating (not SEER2). If you only have a SEER2 rating, multiply it by 1.04 to approximate SEER1 (e.g. SEER2 15.2 → enter 15.8).
data_description.hvac_power_default_watts: Fallback power estimate when no HVAC power sensor is available. Set to your system's typical draw.
data_description.electricity_rate_entity: A sensor or input_number that provides your current electricity rate in $/kWh. Accepts sensor entities or helpers like input_number.
data_description.electricity_flat_rate: Flat electricity rate in $/kWh. Only used when no rate entity is configured. Set to 0 to disable.
data_description.carbon_weight: How much to weigh carbon intensity in optimization decisions (0 = ignore, 1 = prioritize equally with energy efficiency).
data_description.cost_weight: How much to weigh electricity cost in optimization decisions (0 = ignore, 1 = prioritize equally with energy efficiency).
data_description.tou_schedule:
Time-of-use electricity rates let the optimizer shift HVAC runtime to cheaper hours.

Paste a JSON array like this example (weekday afternoon peak at $0.35/kWh, everything else at $0.12/kWh):
['{'"days": [0,1,2,3,4], "start_hour": 14, "end_hour": 19, "rate": 0.35'}', '{'"days": [0,1,2,3,4,5,6], "start_hour": 0, "end_hour": 24, "rate": 0.12'}']

Days: 0=Monday through 6=Sunday. The optimizer checks rates from first to last and uses the first match, so put specific periods before general ones. Leave empty to use the rate entity or flat rate instead.
data_description.demand_response_entity: An input_boolean or binary sensor that, when turned on, triggers demand response mode — widening the comfort range to reduce HVAC load during grid stress events.

solar_panels.title: Solar Panels
solar_panels.description: Configure solar panel production sensors and panel specifications. Panel area and efficiency allow the optimizer to derive solar irradiance from production data.

data_description.solar_production_entity: A sensor reporting current solar panel output in watts.
data_description.grid_import_entity: A sensor reporting current grid import in watts.
data_description.solar_export_rate_entity: A sensor reporting the current solar export rate in $/kWh.
data_description.solar_panel_area: Total surface area of all panels. Auto-calculated if per-panel area and count are provided.
data_description.solar_panel_area_each: Surface area of a single panel.
data_description.solar_panel_efficiency: Panel conversion efficiency (0.05–0.35). Modern residential panels are typically 0.18–0.22.

behavior.title: Optimizer Behavior
behavior.description: Tune how aggressively the optimizer shifts your HVAC runtime and how it handles manual overrides.

data_description.optimization_aggressiveness: Conservative uses the inner 60% of your comfort range. Balanced uses the full range. Aggressive reoptimizes more often and allows larger corrections.
data_description.override_grace_period_hours: How long to pause optimization after you manually adjust the thermostat.
data_description.reoptimize_interval_hours: How often to rebuild the schedule from the latest forecast.
data_description.max_setpoint_change_per_hour: Limits how quickly the optimizer can change your setpoint to avoid jarring temperature swings.
data_description.use_adaptive_model: Enables online learning of your home's thermal characteristics via Extended Kalman Filter.
data_description.use_greybox_model: Uses a physics-based LP optimizer with learned parameters. Requires the adaptive model.
data_description.thermostat_deadband: The temperature deadband (hysteresis) your thermostat uses. Used by the counterfactual simulator to model baseline behavior. Most thermostats use 0.5°F.
data_description.dwell_time_minutes: Minimum time between setpoint writes (5-30 min). Increase if your thermostat reacts poorly to frequent changes.

comfort.title: Temperature Boundaries
comfort.description:
The optimizer saves energy by shifting when your HVAC runs.

COOLING: pre-cool toward the minimum during efficient hours, coast up toward the maximum during expensive hours.
HEATING: pre-heat toward the maximum during efficient hours, coast down toward the minimum during expensive hours.

A wider range means more savings. Safety limits are absolute guardrails that are never exceeded.

data_description.safety_heat_min: Absolute floor. Your home will never be cooler than this, even when away or during demand response. Example: 45°F.
data_description.safety_cool_max: Absolute ceiling. Your home will never be warmer than this, even when away or during demand response. Example: 90°F.
data_description.comfort_cool_min: Pre-cool target during efficient hours (e.g. cool mornings). Example: 72°F.
data_description.comfort_cool_max: How warm you'll accept during expensive/inefficient afternoon hours. Example: 78°F.
data_description.comfort_heat_min: How cool you'll accept during expensive/inefficient hours. Example: 62°F.
data_description.comfort_heat_max: Pre-heat target during efficient hours (e.g. mild afternoons). Example: 70°F.

sleep_schedule.title: Sleep Schedule
sleep_schedule.description: Optional tighter comfort bounds during sleeping hours. When enabled, the optimizer uses these narrower ranges at night so it won't pre-heat or pre-cool aggressively while you're sleeping.

data_description.sleep_schedule_start: When to switch to sleep comfort bounds (e.g. 22:00).
data_description.sleep_schedule_end: When to switch back to daytime comfort bounds (e.g. 07:00).
data_description.sleep_comfort_cool_min: How cool the optimizer will target at night when cooling. Example: 70°F.
data_description.sleep_comfort_cool_max: How warm you'll accept at night when cooling. Example: 76°F.
data_description.sleep_comfort_heat_min: How cool you'll accept at night when heating. Example: 60°F.
data_description.sleep_comfort_heat_max: How warm the optimizer will target at night when heating. Example: 66°F.

presence.title: Presence & Away
presence.description: Configure presence detection to widen the optimization range when you're away.

data_description.occupancy_entities: Person entities, binary sensors, or device trackers used to detect if someone is home.
data_description.occupancy_debounce_minutes: How long to wait after the last sensor reports 'away' before switching to away mode.
data_description.away_comfort_delta: How much wider to make the optimization range when everyone is away.

schedule.title: Calendar Scheduling & Pre-Conditioning
schedule.description: Use calendar entities to forecast departure and arrival patterns for your household. The optimizer applies wider comfort ranges when everyone is away and pre-conditions your home efficiently before the first person returns. Add one calendar per household member for best results.

data_description.calendar_entities: One calendar per household member whose events indicate their location (e.g., 'Remote', 'In-Person'). Events from all calendars are merged to build the occupancy forecast.
data_description.calendar_away_keywords: Comma-separated keywords in event titles that mean someone is away (e.g., 'Office, In-Person, On-Site').
data_description.preconditioning_buffer_minutes: How many minutes before your expected arrival to have the house at the home comfort range.
data_description.departure_trigger_window_minutes: How many minutes before the calendar event ends to start watching for zone departure.
data_description.configure_departures: Pair each household member with their work zone and travel time sensor for accurate pre-conditioning.
data_description.show_advanced: Configure home keywords and the default occupancy mode when no calendar event is active.

schedule_departures.title: Departure Profiles
schedule_departures.description:
Pair each household member with their work zone and travel time sensor. When someone leaves their zone, their specific travel sensor is used to estimate arrival time. Fields are shown in pairs per person.

{person_list}

schedule_advanced.title: Advanced Calendar Settings
schedule_advanced.description: These settings are only needed if your calendar contains events that indicate when you're home (e.g., 'WFH' or 'Remote'). Most users only need away keywords — the optimizer assumes you're home when there's no away event.

data_description.calendar_default_mode: Occupancy mode to assume when there is no calendar event. Most users should leave this as 'home'.
data_description.calendar_home_keywords: Comma-separated keywords in event titles that mean someone is home (e.g., 'WFH, Remote, Home'). Only useful if your default mode is 'away'.

appliance_preset.title: Choose Appliance Type
appliance_preset.description:
Pick a common appliance type to pre-fill typical values, or choose Custom to enter your own.

Each preset provides default thermal impact (BTU/hr) and power draw estimates. You'll configure the state entity (the sensor that tells HA when it's running) on the next screen.

appliance_device.title: Select Device
appliance_device.description: Pick the device to monitor. Its state and power entities will be detected automatically. Toggle 'Configure manually' to skip device selection.

appliance_edit.title: Configure Appliance
appliance_edit.description: Configure the appliance details. For most appliances, just provide the power draw — thermal impact is calculated automatically. All electrical energy consumed by an appliance inside your home becomes heat.

data_description.name: A short name for this appliance (e.g., "Water Heater", "Dryer").
data_description.state_entity: The entity that reports whether this appliance is running — a binary_sensor, switch, water_heater, or sensor.
data_description.active_states: Comma-separated state values that mean "running". Default: on. For sensors with specific states, use the exact string (e.g., "compressor_running").
data_description.power_entity: A sensor reporting real-time power in watts (e.g., from a smart plug). When available, live readings drive the thermal model for more accurate predictions.
data_description.estimated_watts: Power draw when running. This is the primary input for thermal impact calculation. If you have a power sensor, this serves as a fallback.
data_description.thermal_factor: BTU per watt of thermal impact. 3.412 is correct for any appliance fully inside your home (all electricity becomes heat). Negative values mean cooling (e.g., -9.14 for a heat pump water heater). Presets handle special cases automatically.

indoor_sensing.title: Indoor Sensing
indoor_sensing.description: Configure how the optimizer reads indoor temperature and humidity. Simple mode uses a flat list of sensors. Room-Aware mode discovers rooms from your Home Assistant areas and lets you assign sensors per room with occupancy-based weighting.

indoor_simple.title: Indoor Sensors
indoor_simple.description: Select indoor temperature and humidity sensors. The optimizer averages all selected sensors for a whole-house reading.

indoor_rooms.title: Room-Aware Settings
indoor_rooms.description: Configure how room occupancy affects temperature weighting. After setting the mode, you'll select rooms and assign sensors.

data_description.indoor_weighting_mode: Equal: all rooms averaged equally. Occupied only: only rooms with detected motion contribute. Weighted: occupied rooms count more but unoccupied rooms still contribute.
data_description.room_occupancy_debounce_minutes: How long a room stays 'occupied' after the last motion is detected. Longer values prevent flickering from hallway sensors.
data_description.occupied_weight_multiplier: How much more occupied rooms count vs unoccupied. Only used in Weighted mode.

indoor_rooms_discover.title: Select Rooms
indoor_rooms_discover.description: These rooms were discovered from your Home Assistant area registry. Each room shows its detected sensors. Select the rooms to include, then assign sensors on the next page.

indoor_rooms_no_areas.title: No Rooms Found
indoor_rooms_no_areas.description:
No areas with temperature sensors were found in your Home Assistant area registry.

To use room-aware sensing:
1. Go to Settings → Areas in Home Assistant
2. Create areas for your rooms (e.g., Living Room, Bedroom)
3. Assign your temperature sensors to the correct areas
4. Return here to configure room-aware sensing

Press Submit to continue without room-aware sensing.

indoor_rooms_edit.title: Room Sensors
indoor_rooms_edit.description: Assign temperature, humidity, and motion sensors to each room. Discovered sensors are pre-filled from your area registry — adjust as needed. Rooms: {room_details}

blend_mitigation.title: Thermostat Sensor Blending
blend_mitigation.description: Some smart thermostats (Ecobee, Nest) invisibly blend their temperature reading toward an occupied satellite sensor. At night this can shift the reported temperature 5-6°F from the actual room reading, corrupting the thermal model. Choose a mitigation strategy below, or leave disabled if you don't use satellite sensors.

data_description.blend_mitigation_mode: Disabled: no mitigation. Occupancy-Based: excludes thermostat when its area is unoccupied but people are home. Time Schedule: excludes thermostat during a fixed window. Multi-Sensor Median: pools all sensors and excludes outliers.
data_description.thermostat_occupancy_entity: The binary sensor from your thermostat that reports room occupancy (e.g., Ecobee occupancy). Only used in Occupancy-Based mode.
data_description.blend_schedule_start: Time to start suppressing the thermostat reading (e.g., 22:00). Only used in Time Schedule mode.
data_description.blend_schedule_end: Time to stop suppressing the thermostat reading (e.g., 08:00). Only used in Time Schedule mode.
data_description.blend_outlier_threshold_f: How far a sensor can deviate from the median before being excluded. Lower values are stricter. Only used in Multi-Sensor Median mode.

humidity_squelch.title: Wet Room Squelch
humidity_squelch.description: If a temperature sensor is in a bathroom or laundry room, pair it with a co-located humidity sensor. The temperature and humidity sensors will be excluded from indoor averages when humidity spikes (e.g., during showers). Activates at 65% RH, deactivates at 55% RH (hysteresis).

data_description.squelch_temp_entity: The indoor temperature sensor in the wet room (bathroom, laundry). It will be excluded from the indoor average when the paired humidity sensor indicates a moisture event.
data_description.squelch_humidity_entity: The humidity sensor in the same room. When it exceeds 65% RH, the paired temperature sensor is squelched until humidity drops below 55% RH.

error.sensor_overlap: A sensor cannot be used as both indoor and outdoor — please remove the duplicate
error.cool_range_inverted: Cooling minimum must be less than cooling maximum
error.heat_range_inverted: Heating minimum must be less than heating maximum
error.safety_above_comfort: Safety minimum must be below the comfort heating minimum
error.comfort_above_safety: Comfort cooling maximum must be below the safety maximum
error.greybox_requires_adaptive: The grey-box LP optimizer requires the adaptive thermal model to be enabled
error.tou_invalid_json: Invalid JSON — check syntax and try again
error.tou_not_array: TOU schedule must be a JSON array of rate period objects
error.tou_invalid_entry: Each TOU entry must be a JSON object with at least a "rate" field
error.tou_missing_rate: Each TOU entry must include a "rate" field ($/kWh)

force_reoptimize.name: Force Re-optimization
force_reoptimize.description: Immediately re-run the schedule optimizer with the latest forecast.

pause.name: Pause Optimization
pause.description: Pause active optimization and stop writing setpoints to the thermostat.

resume.name: Resume Optimization
resume.description: Resume optimization after a manual pause.

set_occupancy.name: Set Occupancy Mode
set_occupancy.description: Override the occupancy detection with a specific mode.

mode.name: Mode
mode.description: The occupancy mode to set (home, away, vacation, or auto).

demand_response.name: Demand Response
demand_response.description: Activate or deactivate demand response mode to reduce HVAC load.

mode.name: Mode
mode.description: Whether to reduce load or restore normal operation.

duration_minutes.name: Duration (minutes)
duration_minutes.description: How long to maintain demand response mode before auto-restoring.

export_model.name: Export Learned Model
export_model.description: Export the current learned thermal model parameters as JSON.

import_model.name: Import Learned Model
import_model.description: Import previously exported thermal model parameters.

model_data.name: Model Data
model_data.description: The model data dictionary previously returned by export_model.

set_constraint.name: Set Constraint
set_constraint.description: Apply a temporary constraint from an external energy manager or automation.

type.name: Constraint type
type.description: The type of constraint: max_temp, min_temp, max_power, or pause_until.

value.name: Value
value.description: The constraint value (temperature in °F, power in watts, or ignored for pause_until).

duration_minutes.name: Duration (minutes)
duration_minutes.description: How long the constraint remains active before auto-expiring.

source.name: Source
source.description: Identifier for the integration or automation that set this constraint.

forecast_unavailable.title: Weather forecast unavailable
forecast_unavailable.description:
All configured weather entities have been unavailable for an extended period. The optimizer has entered safe mode and is holding your thermostat at a comfortable midpoint.

Check that your weather integration is working in **Settings → Devices & Services**.

thermostat_unavailable.title: Thermostat unavailable
thermostat_unavailable.description:
Your thermostat ({entity_id}) has been unreachable for {minutes} minutes. The optimizer cannot control your HVAC until the connection is restored.

Check your thermostat's connectivity and its integration in **Settings → Devices & Services**.

model_not_progressing.title: Thermal model not learning
model_not_progressing.description:
The thermal model has been learning for {days} days but confidence is only {confidence}%. This usually means the HVAC system hasn't run enough heating/cooling cycles.

This can happen during mild weather when neither heating nor cooling is needed. The model will resume learning when your HVAC system becomes active again.

outdoor_temp_stale.title: Outdoor temperature sensor stale
outdoor_temp_stale.description:
Your outdoor temperature sensor hasn't reported a fresh reading recently. The optimizer is using forecast data as a fallback.

Check the sensor's battery or connectivity in **Settings → Devices & Services**.

indoor_sensors_diverging.title: Indoor temperature sensors disagree
indoor_sensors_diverging.description:
Your indoor temperature sensors disagree by {spread}°F. This may indicate a faulty sensor or a sensor placed near a heat source.

Check your sensors in **Settings → Devices & Services** and verify their placement.

door_window_sensor_unavailable.title: Door/window sensor unavailable
door_window_sensor_unavailable.description:
One or more configured door/window sensors are unavailable: {entities}. The optimizer cannot detect infiltration from these openings until connectivity is restored.

Check the sensor's battery or connectivity in **Settings → Devices & Services**.

current_phase.name: Current Phase
current_phase.description: What the optimizer is doing right now: pre-cooling, coasting, maintaining, or idle.

target_setpoint.name: Target Setpoint
target_setpoint.description: The temperature the optimizer wants your thermostat set to right now.

next_action.name: Next Action
next_action.description: A plain-English summary of the next scheduled temperature change and when it will happen.

predicted_indoor_temp.name: Predicted Indoor Temp
predicted_indoor_temp.description: Where the thermal model thinks your indoor temperature is heading based on weather, insulation, and HVAC state.

prediction_error.name: Prediction Error
prediction_error.description: How far off the model's last prediction was from reality. Smaller is better.

model_accuracy.name: Prediction Accuracy
model_accuracy.description: Overall accuracy of the thermal model's temperature predictions. Lower values mean better predictions.

estimated_savings.name: Estimated Savings
estimated_savings.description: Percentage of HVAC runtime saved today compared to your old thermostat schedule.

model_confidence.name: Model Confidence
model_confidence.description: How well the optimizer understands your home's thermal behavior. Grows over days and weeks as it observes more heating and cooling cycles.

envelope_r_value.name: Insulation R-Value
envelope_r_value.description: How well your home resists heat flow through walls, roof, and windows. Higher values mean better insulation and less energy needed.

thermal_mass.name: Building Heat Storage
thermal_mass.description: How much heat your home's walls, floors, and furnishings can absorb. Higher values mean temperatures change slowly, which is good for coasting.

cooling_capacity.name: Cooling Capacity
cooling_capacity.description: The maximum cooling output your HVAC system can deliver, estimated from observed performance.

heating_capacity.name: Heating Capacity
heating_capacity.description: The maximum heating output your HVAC system can deliver, estimated from observed performance.

effective_cooling_capacity.name: Effective Cooling Capacity
effective_cooling_capacity.description: Cooling output adjusted for current outdoor temperature. Shows what your system would deliver if it turned on right now.

effective_heating_capacity.name: Effective Heating Capacity
effective_heating_capacity.description: Heating output adjusted for current outdoor temperature. Shows what your system would deliver if it turned on right now.

thermal_mass_temperature.name: Building Mass Temperature
thermal_mass_temperature.description: The estimated temperature of your home's walls and floors. This hidden heat reservoir affects how quickly indoor air temperature changes.

schedule.name: Optimization Schedule
schedule.description: The optimizer's current plan showing when it will pre-heat, pre-cool, or coast over the next 24 hours.

outdoor_temp_source.name: Outdoor Temperature
outdoor_temp_source.description: The outdoor temperature the optimizer is using, sourced from your weather entity or outdoor sensors.

indoor_temp_source.name: Indoor Temperature
indoor_temp_source.description: The indoor temperature reading the optimizer is using, which may be averaged from multiple sensors.

net_hvac_power.name: HVAC Power Draw
net_hvac_power.description: Current electrical power consumption of your HVAC system in watts.

savings_kwh_today.name: Energy Saved Today
savings_kwh_today.description: Kilowatt-hours of electricity saved today compared to what your old thermostat schedule would have used.

savings_kwh_cumulative.name: Energy Saved Total
savings_kwh_cumulative.description: Total kilowatt-hours saved since the optimizer was installed.

savings_cost_today.name: Cost Saved Today
savings_cost_today.description: Money saved on your electricity bill today by running HVAC more efficiently.

savings_cost_cumulative.name: Cost Saved Total
savings_cost_cumulative.description: Total money saved since the optimizer was installed.

savings_co2_today.name: CO2 Avoided Today
savings_co2_today.description: Grams of carbon dioxide emissions avoided today by using less electricity or shifting to cleaner grid hours.

savings_co2_cumulative.name: CO2 Avoided Total
savings_co2_cumulative.description: Total carbon dioxide emissions avoided since the optimizer was installed.

baseline_kwh_today.name: Baseline Energy Today
baseline_kwh_today.description: How much energy your old thermostat schedule would have used today without the optimizer.

worst_case_kwh_today.name: Worst Case Energy Today
worst_case_kwh_today.description: Maximum possible energy usage if HVAC ran continuously all day. Used as an upper bound for savings calculations.

occupancy_forecast.name: Occupancy Forecast
occupancy_forecast.description: The optimizer's prediction of when people will be home or away, based on presence sensors and calendar events.

preconditioning_status.name: Pre-conditioning Status
preconditioning_status.description: Whether the optimizer is warming or cooling your home in advance of someone's arrival.

apparent_temperature.name: Feels-Like Temperature
apparent_temperature.description: Indoor temperature adjusted for humidity. High humidity makes warm air feel hotter; low humidity makes cool air feel colder.

occupied_rooms.name: Occupied Rooms
occupied_rooms.description: Which rooms currently have detected occupancy. Used to weight temperature readings toward rooms people are actually in.

weighted_indoor_temp.name: Weighted Indoor Temp
weighted_indoor_temp.description: Indoor temperature averaged across rooms, with occupied rooms weighted more heavily than empty ones.

runtime_savings_today.name: Runtime Savings Today
runtime_savings_today.description: Energy saved today by running the HVAC system fewer total minutes than your old schedule would have.

cop_savings_today.name: Efficiency Savings Today
cop_savings_today.description: Energy saved today by running the HVAC during hours when the compressor operates more efficiently (milder outdoor temperatures).

rate_savings_today.name: Rate Shift Savings Today
rate_savings_today.description: Money saved today by shifting HVAC runtime to hours with cheaper electricity rates.

carbon_shift_savings_today.name: Carbon Shift Savings Today
carbon_shift_savings_today.description: CO2 avoided today by shifting HVAC runtime to hours when the electrical grid was producing less carbon.

baseline_avg_cop.name: Baseline Efficiency (COP)
baseline_avg_cop.description: The average efficiency your HVAC would have achieved today under your old schedule. COP = units of heating/cooling per unit of electricity.

optimized_avg_cop.name: Optimized Efficiency (COP)
optimized_avg_cop.description: The average efficiency your HVAC actually achieved today with optimization. Higher is better.

cop_improvement_pct.name: Efficiency Improvement
cop_improvement_pct.description: Percentage improvement in HVAC efficiency compared to your old thermostat schedule.

comfort_hours_gained.name: Comfort Hours Gained
comfort_hours_gained.description: Additional hours your home stayed within the comfort range today compared to what your old schedule would have achieved.

baseline_comfort_violations.name: Baseline Comfort Violations
baseline_comfort_violations.description: Number of times your old thermostat schedule would have let the temperature drift outside the comfort range today.

baseline_avg_indoor_temp.name: Baseline Avg Indoor Temp
baseline_avg_indoor_temp.description: The average indoor temperature your old schedule would have maintained today.

baseline_confidence.name: Baseline Confidence
baseline_confidence.description: How well the optimizer has captured your old thermostat routine. Needs 7 days of observation to complete.

savings_accuracy_tier.name: Savings Accuracy
savings_accuracy_tier.description: How reliable the savings numbers are: Learning (no data yet), Estimated, Simulated (digital twin active), or Calibrated (fully confident).

profiler_status.name: Performance Profiler
profiler_status.description: Status of the performance profiler that measures your HVAC's actual heating/cooling rates at different outdoor temperatures.

learning_progress.name: Learning Progress
learning_progress.description: Detailed status of the thermal model's learning process, including which parameters are still converging.

current_cop.name: Current Efficiency
current_cop.description: How efficiently your HVAC is operating right now relative to mild conditions. Higher values mean the system is running at a better time.

cop_range.name: Efficiency Spread
cop_range.description: The ratio between the best and worst efficiency in today's forecast. A higher spread means more opportunity for the optimizer to save energy by time-shifting.

optimization_narrative.name: Optimization Status
optimization_narrative.description: A plain-English explanation of what the optimizer is currently doing and why, including efficiency context and forecast information.

appliance_thermal_load.name: Appliance Thermal Load
appliance_thermal_load.description: Net heating or cooling effect of tracked appliances (water heater, dryer, etc.) on your indoor temperature right now.

active_appliances.name: Active Appliances
active_appliances.description: Which tracked appliances are currently running and affecting indoor temperature.

aux_heat_threshold.name: Aux Heat Threshold
aux_heat_threshold.description: The outdoor temperature below which your system tends to activate auxiliary/emergency heat. Learned automatically from observed events.

aux_heat_kwh_today.name: Aux Heat Energy Today
aux_heat_kwh_today.description: Energy consumed by auxiliary/emergency resistive heat strips today. This is expensive backup heat the optimizer tries to avoid.

avoided_aux_heat_kwh_today.name: Avoided Aux Heat Today
avoided_aux_heat_kwh_today.description: Energy that would have been consumed by auxiliary heat strips if not for the optimizer pre-heating your home earlier.

cross_sensor_spread.name: Thermostat Sensor Spread
cross_sensor_spread.description: The temperature difference between your thermostat's built-in sensor and your independent sensors. Large spreads may indicate satellite sensor blending.

optimizer_active.name: Optimizer Active
optimizer_active.description: Whether the optimizer is actively controlling your thermostat right now.

override_detected.name: Override Detected
override_detected.description: Someone manually changed the thermostat. The optimizer will pause for the grace period before resuming.

sensor_stale.name: Sensor Stale
sensor_stale.description: The thermostat's temperature reading hasn't changed in over 24 hours, which may indicate a sensor problem.

aux_heat_active.name: Aux Heat Active
aux_heat_active.description: Auxiliary/emergency resistive heat is currently running. This is expensive backup heat that activates in very cold conditions.

learning_active.name: Learning Active
learning_active.description: The thermal model is still learning your home's characteristics. Optimization improves as confidence grows.

thermostat_blend_suspected.name: Thermostat Blend Suspected
thermostat_blend_suspected.description: The thermostat may be invisibly blending its temperature reading with a satellite sensor, which can confuse the thermal model.

enabled.name: Optimizer Enabled
enabled.description: Master switch to enable or disable the optimizer. When off, the thermostat holds its current setpoint.
